channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Applied Explainability for Large Language Models: A Comparative Study
item.description: arXiv:2604.15371v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) achieve strong performance across many natural language processing tasks, yet their decision processes remain difficult to interpret. This lack of transparency creates challenges for trust, debugging, and deployment in rea...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, arxiv, attention, model, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Why Fine-Tuning Encourages Hallucinations and How to Fix It
item.description: arXiv:2604.15574v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models are prone to hallucinating factually incorrect statements. A key source of these errors is exposure to new factual information through supervised fine-tuning (SFT), which can increase hallucinations w.r.t. knowledge acquired duri...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> product, arxiv, fine-tuning, model, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: "Excuse me, may I say something..." CoLabScience, A Proactive AI Assistant for Biomedical Discovery and LLM-Expert Collaborations
item.description: arXiv:2604.15588v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) into scientific workflows presents exciting opportunities to accelerate biomedical discovery. However, the reactive nature of LLMs, which respond only when prompted, limits their effectiveness in collabo...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, model, study, RAG, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LLM attribution analysis across different fine-tuning strategies and model scales for automated code compliance
item.description: arXiv:2604.15589v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Existing research on large language models (LLMs) for automated code compliance has primarily focused on performance, treating the models as black boxes and overlooking how training decisions affect their interpretive behavior. This paper addresses th...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, model, analysis, LLM, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LLMs Corrupt Your Documents When You Delegate
item.description: arXiv:2604.15597v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are poised to disrupt knowledge work, with the emergence of delegated work as a new interaction paradigm (e.g., vibe coding). Delegation requires trust - the expectation that the LLM will faithfully execute the task withou...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, analysis, study, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LACE: Lattice Attention for Cross-thread Exploration
item.description: arXiv:2604.15529v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Current large language models reason in isolation. Although it is common to sample multiple reasoning paths in parallel, these trajectories do not interact, and often fail in the same redundant ways. We introduce LACE, a framework that transforms reas...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> attention, model, large language model, reasoning, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Bilevel Optimization of Agent Skills via Monte Carlo Tree Search
item.description: arXiv:2604.15709v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agent \texttt{skills} are structured collections of instructions, tools, and supporting resources that help large language model (LLM) agents perform particular classes of tasks. Empirical evidence shows that the design of \texttt{skills} can material...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> instruction, arxiv, model, LLM, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Aletheia: Gradient-Guided Layer Selection for Efficient LoRA Fine-Tuning Across Architectures
item.description: arXiv:2604.15351v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) has become the dominant parameter-efficient fine-tuning method for large language models, yet standard practice applies LoRA adapters uniformly to all transformer layers regardless of their relevance to the downstream task. ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, fine-tuning, model, large language model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Mapping High-Performance Regions in Battery Scheduling across Data Uncertainty, Battery Design, and Planning Horizons
item.description: arXiv:2604.15360v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This study presents a triadic analysis of energy storage operation under multi-stage model predictive control, investigating the interplay between data characteristics, forecast uncertainty, planning horizon, and battery c-rate. Synthetic datasets are...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, analysis, study, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Brain Score Tracks Shared Properties of Languages: Evidence from Many Natural Languages and Structured Sequences
item.description: arXiv:2604.15503v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent breakthroughs in language models (LMs) using neural networks have raised the question: how similar are these models' processing to human language processing? Results using a framework called Brain Score (BS) -- predicting fMRI activations durin...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, experiment, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Preregistered Belief Revision Contracts
item.description: arXiv:2604.15558v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deliberative multi-agent systems allow agents to exchange messages and revise beliefs over time. While this interaction is meant to improve performance, it can also create dangerous conformity effects: agreement, confidence, prestige, or majority size...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, vision, ICL, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
